da.hero_subtitle: En ærlig, dybdegående guide til at vælge det rigtige format, den rigtige sæson og den rigtige destination for din yogalæreruddannelse.
da.toc_subtitle: Denne side er skrevet som et fagligt opslagsværk — ikke en salgstale. Vi dækker fakta, fordele og ulemper, så du kan træffe et informeret valg.
da.toc1_desc: 18-ugers fleksibel (hverdag vs. weekend), 8-ugers semi-intensiv og 4-ugers intensiv — med ærlige fordele og ulemper.
da.toc2_desc: Hvornår er det bedst at tage sin YTT? Forår, sommer, efterår eller vinter — og hvorfor det betyder noget.
da.toc3_desc: Bali, Indien, Thailand, Costa Rica, Portugal og København — ærlig sammenligning af de mest populære YTT-destinationer.
da.toc4_desc: Et beslutningsrammeværk baseret på din livssituation, mål, budget og personlighed.
da.s1_subtitle: Yoga Bible tilbyder tre formater for 200-timers yogalæreruddannelse. Curriculum, certificering og pris er den samme (25.500 DKK) — forskellen er tempo, tidsfordeling og livsstilskrav.
da.f18wd_subtitle: Det mest populære format for københavnere med job eller studie.
da.f18wd_img_alt: Yogalærerstuderende træner i studiet på en hverdag i København
da.f18wd_p1: Beholde job eller studie: Du kan arbejde morgen/aften og i de 4–5 ugedage uden undervisning
da.f18wd_p2: Lang integrationsperiode: 18 uger giver tid til at absorbere og praktisere materialet mellem sessioner
da.f18wd_p3: Øve i hverdagen: Du kan undervise venner, gå til klasser og øve cueing løbende
da.f18wd_p4: Minimal livsstilsændring: Du bor hjemme, beholder dine rutiner, og bygger gradvist nye vaner
da.f18wd_p5: Tid til refleksion: Filosofi og anatomi kræver fordøjelsestid — 18 uger giver den
da.f18wd_p6: Netværk over tid: Du opbygger relationer med medstuderende og lokalsamfundet over flere måneder
da.f18wd_c1: Langt commitment: 4½ måneds kalenderblokering kan føles langtrukket for nogle
da.f18wd_c2: Risiko for afbrydelser: Sygdom, rejser, arbejdspres — over 18 uger er der flere muligheder for konflikter
da.f18wd_c3: Mindre immersivt: Du går "ind og ud" af uddannelsen — ikke fuldt dybt i yogaens verden
da.f18wd_c4: Kræver selvdisciplin: Selvstudietimer kræver egen motivation i en travl hverdag
da.f18wd_c5: Hverdagstimerne (10–15) passer ikke alle: Skiftehold, faste kontortider eller børnepasning kan kollidere
da.f18we_subtitle: Designet til dig med fuldtidsjob mandag–fredag.
da.f18we_img_alt: Weekend yogalæreruddannelse i København — studerende i weekend session
da.f18we_p1: Fuld kompatibilitet med fuldtidsarbejde: Mandag–fredag er fri — ingen kollision med kontortider
da.f18we_p2: Intensive weekendblokke: Lørdag–søndag 09–17 giver fordybelse i weekenderne
da.f18we_p3: Samme curriculum og certificering: Intet kompromis på indhold
da.f18we_p4: Community-effekt: Weekendholdet udvikler ofte stærke bånd (delt opofrelse af fritid)
da.f18we_p5: Hverdage til øvelse: Du kan øve hjemme eller gå til klasser på hverdage
da.f18we_c1: Alle dine weekender er optaget: 18 weekender i træk — ingen spontane weekend-rejser eller sociale planer
da.f18we_c2: Mental udmattelse: Fuld arbejdsuge + fuld weekend-uddannelse = potentiel udbrændthed
da.f18we_c3: Begrænset restitution: Du har reelt ingen "fri" dage i 18 uger
da.f18we_c4: Familieliv kan lide: Partner, børn eller venner ser dig sjældent i weekenderne
da.f18we_c5: Aftensessioner kan overraske: Nogle hverdagsaftener kræves — check det specifikke skema
da.f8w_p1: Kortere tidsramme: 8 uger vs. 18 uger — halvdelen af kalenderblokkeringen
da.f8w_p2: Mere intenst end 18-ugers: 3–4 dage/uge skaber dybere immersion og momentum
da.f8w_p3: Stadig muligt at arbejde deltid: 2–3 dage fri per uge giver plads til deltidsjob
da.f8w_p4: God "flow": Nok tid mellem sessioner til fordybelse, men kort nok til at holde energien
da.f8w_p5: Sommer-timing: Maj/juni-start falder sammen med ferieperioden for mange
da.f8w_p6: Balanceret tempo: Ikke så presset som 4-ugers, ikke så langstrakt som 18-ugers
da.f8w_c1: Højere ugeligt tidskrav: 20–25 timer/uge inkl. selvstudie — sværere at kombinere med fuldtidsjob
da.f8w_c2: Komprimeret stof: Anatomi og filosofi kommer hurtigere — mindre tid til at fordøje komplekse emner
da.f8w_c3: Begrænset fleksibilitet ved fravær: Missede dage er sværere at indhente over kun 8 uger
da.f8w_c4: Færre startdatoer: Kun Maj/Juni 2026 — mindre fleksibilitet i planlægningen
da.f8w_c5: Kræver stærk planlægning: Familiære og sociale forpligtelser skal koordineres tæt
da.f4w_subtitle: Total immersion — fuld tid, 7 dage om ugen.
da.f4w_d4: Timer per uge: ~35–40 kontakttimer + selvstudie om aftenen
da.f4w_d7: Bolig: Valgfri — hjælp med bolig i København tilbydes
da.f4w_p1: Total fordybelse: Du lever, ånder og sover yoga i 4 uger — dybt transformativt
da.f4w_p2: Hurtigste vej til certificering: Færdig på én måned — fra start til RYT-200
da.f4w_p3: Stærke bånd: 4 uger tæt sammen skaber intense venskaber og professionelle netværk
da.f4w_p4: Perfekt for internationale: Rejse til København, tag uddannelsen, rejs hjem certificeret
da.f4w_p5: Klart fokus: Ingen distraktion fra job — du kan give 100% til uddannelsen
da.f4w_p6: Sommersæson (juli): Lange lyse dage, udendørs praksis muligheder, København på sit bedste
da.f4w_p7: Bolighjælp: Yoga Bible hjælper med at finde bolig til internationale studerende
da.f4w_c1: Kræver orlov eller opsigelse: Du kan ikke arbejde eller studere ved siden af — det er fuld tid
da.f4w_c2: Fysisk krævende: 8–18 dagligt, 7 dage om ugen — din krop og sind udfordres
da.f4w_c3: Komprimeret indlæring: 200 timers stof på 4 uger — begrænset tid til fordøjelse af filosofi og anatomi
da.f4w_c4: Risiko for overbelastning: Skader, mental udmattelse og informationsoverload er reelle risici
da.f4w_c5: Mindre tid til at internalisere: Du lærer hurtigt, men den dybe forankring kommer ofte først bagefter
da.f4w_c6: Begrænset fleksibilitet: Sygdom eller personlige hændelser er vanskelige at håndtere med 10% fraværsgrænse
da.f4w_c7: Boligudgift: Internationale studerende skal beregne ekstra 8.000–15.000 DKK for 4 ugers bolig i København
da.table_title: Overblik: Alle Formater Side om Side
da.table_note: Alle formater bruger det samme curriculum, de samme instruktører og giver den samme Yoga Alliance RYT-200 certificering. Den eneste forskel er tempo og tidsfordeling.
da.s2_title: Del 2: Hvornår Skal Du Tage Din YTT?
da.s2_subtitle: Tidspunktet for din yogalæreruddannelse påvirker din oplevelse mere end de fleste regner med. Her er en ærlig gennemgang af sæsonerne — både i København og generelt.
da.s2_img_alt: København gennem fire årstider — forår, sommer, efterår, vinter
da.spring_b1: Dagslyset vokser markant: Fra 10 timer i marts til 17 timer i maj — du mærker bogstaveligt talt energien stige uge for uge
da.spring_b2: Byen vågner: Kanalerne, parkerne og havnebadene begynder at leve — god energi for en ny start
da.spring_b3: Perfekt for 18-ugers forløb: Start i marts, afslut i juli/august — din uddannelse følger lysets naturlige kurve
da.spring_b4: Lavere boligpriser: Turismesæsonen er ikke startet endnu — internationale studerende finder lettere bolig
da.spring_b5: Overvejelse: Marts–april kan stadig være koldt og gråt. Mentalt kræver det tålmodighed
da.summer_b1: Længste dage i Skandinavien: Op til 17 timers dagslys — energi til praksis, socialitet og udforskning
da.summer_b2: Udendørs muligheder: Praksis i parker, ved kanaler, på tage — København tilbyder unikke outdoor spaces
da.summer_b3: Bedst for 4-ugers intensiv: Juli-holdet giver den ultimative sommeroplevelse i København
da.summer_b4: Feriestemning: Mange danskere er på ferie — byen er rolig men levende. God til fordybelse
da.summer_b5: Overvejelse: Højsæson = dyrere bolig og flere turister. Book bolig i god tid
da.summer_b6: Overvejelse: Varme dage i et hot yoga-studie kræver ekstra hydrering og hvile
da.autumn_b1: Indadvendt energi: Dagslyset falder — naturligt match med filosofi, meditation og selvstudie
da.autumn_b2: Byen er i gang: Studerende, professionelle og kulturliv er på fuld kraft efter sommerferien
da.autumn_b3: Gode studieforhold: Mindre distration fra udendørs aktiviteter — fokus på det indre arbejde
da.autumn_b4: Overvejelse: Mørke eftermiddage (mørkt kl. 16 i november) kan påvirke humør og energi
da.autumn_b5: Overvejelse: Regn og blæst er hyppige — cykling og transport kan føles mere besværligt
da.winter_b1: Mest introvert sæson: Korte dage og koldt vejr skaber naturlig ramme for dybt indre arbejde
da.winter_b2: Hyggesæson: Stearinlys, varme drikke og intime samlinger — den danske hygge-kultur er på sit stærkeste
da.winter_b3: Billigste bolig: Laveste lejepriser i København — godt for internationales budget
da.winter_b4: Hot yoga føles ekstra godt: At gå fra frost ind i et varmt studie er en sanseoplevelse i sig selv
da.winter_b5: Overvejelse: Ned til 7 timers dagslys — kræver bevidst lysterapi og D-vitamin
da.winter_b6: Overvejelse: Vinterkulden er mild men våd — glat og gråt vejr i perioder
da.season_rec_text: Der er ingen objektivt "bedste" sæson — det afhænger af din livssituation, personlighed og mål. Hvis du er energi-sensitiv og trives med lys og varme, er forår/sommer ideelt. Hvis du søger dybde, ro og introspection, kan efterår/vinter overraske dig positivt. Det vigtigste er, at tidspunktet passer til dit liv — ikke at det ser perfekt ud på Instagram.
da.s3_subtitle: Tusinder af mennesker vælger hvert år at tage deres yogalæreruddannelse i udlandet. Her gennemgår vi de mest populære destinationer med ærlige styrker og svagheder — inklusiv København, som vi selvfølgelig kender bedst, men forsøger at vurdere lige så kritisk.
da.bali_subtitle: Den mest populære YTT-destination i verden — og med god grund, men også med nuancer.
da.bali_img_alt: Yoga i Bali — risterrasser og udendørs praksis i Ubud
da.bali_s1: Pris: YTT fra $2.000–3.500 USD inkl. bolig og mad — markant billigere end Vesten
da.bali_s2: Spirituel atmosfære: Ubud og omegn er gennemsyret af yoga- og wellness-kultur
da.bali_s3: Klima: Varmt tropisk klima året rundt (25–33°C) — perfekt for udendørs praksis
da.bali_s4: Fællesskab: Enormt internationalt yogamiljø med workshops, retreats og kirtan dagligt
da.bali_s5: Total immersion: Du er "ude af din hverdag" — ingen distraktion fra job eller rutiner
da.bali_s6: Natur: Risterrasser, vulkaner, vandfald og templer — visuelt inspirerende
da.bali_w1: Kvalitetsvariaton: Markedet er overmættet — mange billige YTT'er med tvivlsom kvalitet og uerfarne undervisere
da.bali_w2: Turist-boble: Yogamiljøet i Ubud kan føles kommercielt og afkoblet fra lokal kultur
da.bali_w3: Isolation fra virkelighed: Uddannelsen foregår i en "boble" — svært at overføre til undervisning hjemme
da.bali_w4: Vejr-risiko: Regnsæson (november–marts) kan betyde kraftig regn og fugt dagligt
da.bali_w5: Sundhed: Mavetrouble, dehydrering og moskitobårne sygdomme er reelle risici
da.bali_w7: Ikke Yoga Alliance automatisk: Ikke alle Bali-skoler er registrerede — verificer altid skolens RYS-status
da.india_subtitle: Yogaens fødeland. Rishikesh er den historiske hovedstad; Goa tilbyder en mere afslappet variant.
da.india_img_alt: Yoga i Rishikesh, Indien — praksis ved Ganges-floden
da.india_s1: Autenticitet: Rishikesh er bogstaveligt talt "Yogaens Verdenshovedstad" — dybere spirituel kontekst end nogen anden destination
da.india_s2: Pris: YTT fra $1.200–2.500 USD inkl. bolig og mad — den billigste seriøse destination
da.india_s3: Tradition: Adgang til mestre og lineage-baseret undervisning, der rækker generationer tilbage
da.india_s4: Kulturel dybde: Aarti-ceremonier ved Ganges, ashrams, templer — yogafilosofi er levende kultur her
da.india_s5: Vegetarisk mad: Frisk, ayurvedisk mad — ofte det sundeste man har spist
da.india_w1: Kulturelt chok: Indien er overvældende for mange vesterlændinge — larm, trafik, fattigdom og bureaukrati
da.india_w2: Sundhedsrisiko: "Delhi belly" er næsten uundgåeligt. Vandkvalitet og hygiejne kræver konstant opmærksomhed
da.india_w3: Kvalitetsvariaton: Hundredvis af skoler i Rishikesh — mange billige med dårlig infrastruktur og underviserkvalitet
da.india_w4: Komfort: Simpel bolig (delt værelse, basistoilet) — ikke for alle
da.india_w5: Klima: Rishikesh er ekstremt varmt om sommeren (40°C+) og køligt om vinteren. Bedst: oktober–marts
da.india_w6: Visum og logistik: E-visum kræves, og intern transport (Delhi–Rishikesh) kan være kaotisk
da.india_w7: Undervisningsstil: Traditionelle indiske skoler kan have en mere rigid, guru-centreret pædagogik — ikke alle trives med det
da.thailand_subtitle: Strand, jungle og overkommelige priser — en populær mellemvej.
da.thailand_img_alt: Yoga i Thailand — strand-praksis på Koh Phangan
da.thailand_s1: Balance mellem komfort og pris: YTT fra $2.500–4.000 USD — bedre faciliteter end Indien, billigere end Europa
da.thailand_s2: Fantastisk mad: Thailandsk cuisine er verdensberømt — frisk, sund og billig
da.thailand_s3: Infrastruktur: Thailand har god infrastruktur, hurtig wifi, moderne faciliteter
da.thailand_s4: Natur: Strande, jungle, varme kilder — smuk ramme for en transformativ oplevelse
da.thailand_s5: Velkomstkultur: Thailand er generelt sikkert, venligt og nemt at navigere for vesterlændinge
da.thailand_w1: Yoga er importeret: Thailand har ikke en indfødt yogatradition — underviserne er typisk vesterlændinge bosiddende i Asien
da.thailand_w2: Party-kultur: Koh Phangan er også Full Moon Party-øen — distraktioner er reelle
da.thailand_w3: Varme og fugt: Tropisk varme (30–35°C) med høj luftfugtighed kan være udmattende
da.thailand_w4: Kvalitetsspredning: Som Bali — mange skoler, varierende kvalitet. Research er vigtigt
da.thailand_w5: Monsun: Regnsæson (maj–oktober) kan betyde daglig kraftig regn
da.costarica_subtitle: Centralamerikansk jungle, bæredygtighed og "Pura Vida" — den nye populære destination.
da.costarica_img_alt: Yoga i Costa Rica — jungle retreat og udendørs praksis
da.costarica_s1: Natur og biodiversitet: Regnskov, vulkaner, strande — en af de mest naturskønne destinationer
da.costarica_s2: Bæredygtighedskultur: Costa Rica er verdensførende i grøn energi — matcher yogaens etiske værdier
da.costarica_s3: Sikkerhed: Regionen er generelt meget sikker for rejsende
da.costarica_s4: Engelsk-venligt: Mange costaricaner taler engelsk — nemt at navigere
da.costarica_w1: Dyrere end Asien: YTT fra $3.000–5.500 USD — plus dyre flybilletter fra Europa (8.000–12.000 DKK)
da.costarica_w2: Isoleret beliggenhed: Mange retreats er afsides i junglen — fantastisk for immersion, men besværligt ved nødsituationer
da.costarica_w3: Fugt og insekter: Tropisk klima med moskitoer, edderkopper og høj luftfugtighed
da.costarica_w4: Begrænset yoga-tradition: Som Thailand er yoga her importeret — ikke en lokal tradition
da.costarica_w5: Tidsforskel: 7–8 timers tidsforskel fra Danmark — påvirker kontakt med hjemmet
da.portugal_subtitle: Europas yoga-hotspot med sol, surf og sydeuropæisk charme — og ingen lang flyvetur.
da.portugal_img_alt: Yoga i Portugal — kystlandskab og udendørs praksis i Algarve
da.portugal_s1: Kort rejse fra Skandinavien: 3–4 timers flyvning, ingen tidsforskel — nemt at nå og billigt at flyve
da.portugal_s2: Behageligt klima: Mildt middelhavsklima (15–30°C) — ikke for varmt, ikke for koldt
da.portugal_s3: EU-land: Ingen visum, EU-sundhedskort gælder, danske sim-kort fungerer
da.portugal_s4: Gode faciliteter: Moderne retreats med god standard — europæisk komfortniveau
da.portugal_s5: Surf + yoga: Ericeira er Europas surfmekka — populær kombination
da.portugal_w1: Dyrere end Asien: YTT fra €2.500–4.500 — tættere på danske priser
da.portugal_w2: Mindre yoga-tradition: Yogamiljøet er relativt nyt og primært drevet af expats
da.portugal_w3: Sæsonbestemt: Vinteren er mild men regnfuld — bedst i foråret/sommeren
da.portugal_w4: Turistpres: Populære områder (Lagos, Ericeira) kan føles overrendte
da.portugal_w5: Mindre fællesskab efter YTT: Når du rejser hjem, er dit yoga-fællesskab i et andet land
da.cph_subtitle: Din egen by — eller en af Europas mest liveable byer, hvis du rejser hertil.
da.cph_img_alt: Yogalæreruddannelse i København — praksis i Christianshavn studie
da.cph_s1: Ingen rejseomkostninger (lokale): Bor hjemme, spis hjemme, behold dit job — ingen ekstra udgifter ud over uddannelsen
da.cph_s2: Undervis lokalt fra dag 1: Real-class praksis i københavnske studier — du bygger netværk der, hvor du skal undervise
da.cph_s3: Fortsæt dit liv: Familie, venner, job og rutiner fortsætter — uddannelsen integreres i dit liv, ikke omvendt
da.cph_s4: Lokalt netværk: Dine medstuderende er dine fremtidige kollegaer og venner — i din by
da.cph_s5: Fleksible formater: 4, 8 eller 18 uger — tilpasset din livssituation
da.cph_s6: Europæisk by-kvalitet: Verdensklasse gastronomisk, kulturelt og arkitektonisk — mere end bare yoga
da.cph_s7: Sundhedssystem: Dansk/EU sundhedsdækning — ingen bekymring om rejseforsikring
da.cph_s8: Sikkerhed: Konsekvent rangeret som en af verdens sikreste byer
da.cph_s9: Yoga Alliance registreret: RYS-200 status med Yoga Alliance — garanteret certificeringsstandard
da.cph_w1: Ikke eksotisk: Ingen risterrasser, tropiske strande eller ashram-stemning — det er en nordeuropæisk by
da.cph_w2: Vejret: Koldt og mørkt november–februar, uforudsigeligt vejr generelt — ikke drømmevejr for udendørs yoga
da.cph_w3: Dyrere samlet (for internationale): Bolig i København (8.000–15.000 DKK/måned) + leveomkostninger er markant dyrere end Asien
da.cph_w4: Hverdagens distraktion: Du er i din vante omgivelse — fristelsen til at prioritere job, venner og fester kan underminere fordybelse
da.cph_w5: Mindre "transformativ boble": Du får ikke den totale adskillelse fra hverdagen, som et retreat-format tilbyder
da.cph_w6: Begrænset yogascene sammenlignet med storbyer: København har et godt yogamiljø, men det er mindre end London, Berlin eller Los Angeles
da.retreat_title: Det Vigtige Spørgsmål: Retreat vs. Hjemby
da.retreat_subtitle: Det dybeste spørgsmål er ikke "Bali eller København" — det er om du har brug for at forlade din hverdag for at fordybe dig, eller om du vil integrere uddannelsen i dit eksisterende liv.
da.retreat_r1: Du har svært ved at prioritere dig selv i din hverdag
da.retreat_r2: Du ønsker en total "reset" og ny start
da.retreat_r3: Du ikke har planer om at undervise lokalt bagefter
da.retreat_r4: Du har lyst til at rejse OG uddanne dig samtidig
da.retreat_r5: Du trives med isolation og nye omgivelser
da.retreat_r6: Du har budget til rejse, bolig og uddannelse samlet
da.home_r1: Du vil undervise lokalt og bygge netværk fra dag 1
da.home_r2: Du har job, familie eller forpligtelser, du ikke kan slippe
da.home_r3: Du ønsker at integrere yoga i dit eksisterende liv — ikke flygte fra det
da.home_r4: Du vil have real-class undervisningserfaring i rigtige studier
da.home_r5: Du foretrækker et gradvist format (18 uger) frem for 4-ugers intensiv
da.home_r6: Du vil spare på rejse- og boligudgifter
da.s4_title: Del 4: Hvordan Vælger Du Det Rigtige?
da.s4_subtitle: Brug dette rammeværk til at strukturere din beslutning. Der er ingen "rigtig" eller "forkert" — kun hvad der passer bedst til dit liv lige nu.
da.choose1_desc: Vil du undervise professionelt? Prioriter en skole med real-class praksis og lokalt netværk. Er det personlig udvikling? Et retreat kan være mere passende. Begge dele? Vælg et format med balance mellem fordybelse og praktik.
da.choose2_desc: Job: Kan du tage fri? 18-ugers weekend passer fuldtid. 4-ugers kræver orlov. Familie: Har du ansvar, der holder dig hjemme? Økonomi: Har du buffer til 1–4 måneders reduceret indkomst?
da.choose3_desc: Langsom læringstype? 18-ugers format giver tid til fordøjelse. Intensiv læringstype? 4-ugers passer dig bedre. Introvert? Total immersion kan være overvældende. Ekstrovert? Du trives sandsynligvis med et intensivt gruppemiljø.
da.choose4_desc: Total cost-of-ownership: Uddannelsespris + bolig + fly + tabt indkomst + mad + visum. En "billig" YTT i Bali med flybillet, bolig og 4 ugers tabt løn kan være dyrere end en YTT i din hjemby, hvor du beholder dit job.
da.choose5_desc: Tjek altid: Er skolen Yoga Alliance registreret (RYS)? Hvem er underviserne, og hvad er deres baggrund? Findes der anmeldelser fra tidligere studerende? Hvor mange timer er kontakttimer vs. selvstudie? Er der real-class undervisningspraksis inkluderet?
da.choose6_desc: Det vigtigste spørgsmål: Hvad sker der dagen efter du er certificeret? Har du et netværk, et studio at undervise i, et community at vende tilbage til? Eller rejser du hjem til en tom kalender? Det bedste YTT er det, der giver dig bæredygtig start — ikke bare et diplom.
da.insight_p1: Mange nyuddannede yogalærere oplever en "post-YTT-dip": de vender hjem fra et retreat, fyldt med inspiration — men har intet netværk, intet studio og ingen plan. Den transformative oplevelse falmer hurtigt, når hverdagen vender tilbage.
da.insight_p2: Det er ikke et argument mod retreat-formater — det er et argument for at have en plan for hvad der sker bagefter. Uanset om du vælger Bali, Rishikesh eller København, er den vigtigste investering ikke selve uddannelsen — det er de første 6 måneder efter.
da.faq1_q: Hvad er forskellen på curriculum i de forskellige formater?
da.faq1_a: Ingen. Alle Yoga Bible-formater (18-ugers, 8-ugers og 4-ugers) bruger nøjagtigt det samme curriculum, de samme manualer, de samme eksaminer og de samme instruktører. Forskellen er udelukkende tempo og tidsfordeling. Certificeringen (RYT-200) er identisk.
da.faq2_a: I særlige tilfælde (f.eks. sygdom, jobskifte eller familieforhold) kan du anmode om at skifte til et andet format eller færdiggøre i næste hold. Kontakt os tidligt, så finder vi en løsning.
da.faq3_q: Er en YTT i Bali eller Indien "mindre værd" end en i Danmark?
da.faq3_a: Nej. En Yoga Alliance RYT-200 certificering er den samme uanset destination — det er en international standard. Kvaliteten af selve uddannelsen afhænger af skolen, underviserne og programmet — ikke af geografisk placering. Der findes fremragende skoler overalt, og der findes dårlige skoler overalt. Research er afgørende.
da.faq4_q: Hvad koster det samlet at tage en YTT i udlandet vs. København?
da.faq4_a: For en dansker med fuldtidsjob: En 4-ugers YTT i Bali koster typisk ~$3.000 USD (YTT) + ~6.000 DKK (fly) + ~4 ugers tabt løn (~50.000–80.000 DKK). Samlet: 75.000–110.000 DKK. En 18-ugers weekend-YTT i København: 25.500 DKK + 0 kr i tabt løn (du beholder dit job). Samlet: 25.500 DKK. Tallene er omtrentlige og individuelle — men forskellen er markant.
da.faq5_q: Er 4 uger nok til at lære yoga?
da.faq5_a: 200 timer er 200 timer — uanset om de er fordelt over 4 eller 18 uger. Men yogalæreruddannelsen er en begyndelse, ikke en afslutning. Uanset format skal du regne med 6–12 måneder af fortsat praksis og undervisning, før du føler dig tryg og kompetent som underviser.
da.faq6_q: Hvad er forskellen på RYT-200 fra forskellige skoler?
da.faq6_a: Yoga Alliance sætter minimumskrav (200 timer fordelt på specifikke kategorier). Men skoler varierer meget i kvalitet, dybde, underviserstab og praksis. En RYT-200 fra en seriøs skole med erfarne undervisere og real-class praksis er en stærkere kvalifikation end en fra en billig skole uden praktisk undervisningserfaring — selvom diplomet ser ens ud.
da.faq7_q: Kan jeg tage min YTT i sommeren og begynde at undervise til efteråret?
da.faq7_a: Ja — det er en populær plan. Juli 4-ugers intensiv → August hvile og egen praksis → September start med at undervise. Mange studios og fitnesscentre rekrutterer instruktører til efterårssæsonen. Yoga Bible hjælper med networking og anbefalinger til lokale studios.
da.faq8_q: Er det nødvendigt at have en YTT for at undervise yoga?
da.faq8_a: Juridisk: Nej. Yoga er ikke en reguleret profession i Danmark (eller de fleste lande). Praktisk: Ja. De fleste studier, fitnesscentre og forsikringsselskaber kræver RYT-200 som minimum. Og vigtigere: en grundig uddannelse beskytter dine elever mod skader og giver dig troværdighed.
da.cta_text: Se vores tre formater i detaljer, eller kontakt os for personlig vejledning.

en.hero_subtitle: An honest, in-depth guide to choosing the right format, the right season and the right destination for your yoga teacher training.
en.toc_subtitle: This page is written as a professional reference — not a sales pitch. We cover facts, pros and cons, so you can make an informed choice.
en.toc1_desc: 18-week flexible (weekday vs. weekend), 8-week semi-intensive and 4-week intensive — with honest pros and cons.
en.toc2_desc: When is the best time to do your YTT? Spring, summer, autumn or winter — and why it matters.
en.toc3_desc: Bali, India, Thailand, Costa Rica, Portugal and Copenhagen — honest comparison of the most popular YTT destinations.
en.toc4_desc: A decision framework based on your life situation, goals, budget and personality.
en.s1_subtitle: Yoga Bible offers three formats for the 200-hour yoga teacher training. Curriculum, certification and price are the same (25,500 DKK) — the difference is pace, time distribution and lifestyle requirements.
en.f18wd_subtitle: The most popular format for Copenhageners with a job or studies.
en.f18wd_img_alt: Yoga teacher trainees practicing in the studio on a weekday in Copenhagen
en.f18wd_p1: Keep your job or studies: You can work mornings/evenings and on the 4-5 weekdays without classes
en.f18wd_p2: Long integration period: 18 weeks gives time to absorb and practice the material between sessions
en.f18wd_p3: Practice in everyday life: You can teach friends, attend classes and practice cueing continuously
en.f18wd_p4: Minimal lifestyle change: You stay home, keep your routines, and gradually build new habits
en.f18wd_p5: Time for reflection: Philosophy and anatomy need digestion time — 18 weeks provides it
en.f18wd_p6: Networking over time: You build relationships with fellow students and the local community over several months
en.f18wd_c1: Long commitment: 4.5 months of calendar blocking can feel drawn out for some
en.f18wd_c2: Risk of interruptions: Illness, travel, work pressure — over 18 weeks there are more chances for conflicts
en.f18wd_c3: Less immersive: You go "in and out" of the training — not fully deep in the world of yoga
en.f18wd_c4: Requires self-discipline: Self-study hours require your own motivation in a busy everyday life
en.f18wd_c5: Weekday hours (10-15) don't suit everyone: Shift work, fixed office hours or childcare can conflict
en.f18we_subtitle: Designed for those with a full-time job Monday-Friday.
en.f18we_img_alt: Weekend yoga teacher training in Copenhagen — students in weekend session
en.f18we_d5: Hours per week: ~12-16 contact hours on weekends
en.f18we_p1: Full compatibility with full-time work: Monday-Friday is free — no collision with office hours
en.f18we_p2: Intensive weekend blocks: Saturday-Sunday 09-17 provides immersion on weekends
en.f18we_p3: Same curriculum and certification: No compromise on content
en.f18we_p4: Community effect: Weekend groups often develop strong bonds (shared sacrifice of free time)
en.f18we_p5: Weekdays for practice: You can practice at home or attend classes on weekdays
en.f18we_c1: All your weekends are taken: 18 weekends in a row — no spontaneous weekend trips or social plans
en.f18we_c2: Mental exhaustion: Full work week + full weekend training = potential burnout
en.f18we_c3: Limited recovery: You effectively have no "free" days for 18 weeks
en.f18we_c4: Family life may suffer: Partner, children or friends see you rarely on weekends
en.f18we_c5: Evening sessions may surprise: Some weekday evenings are required — check the specific schedule
en.f8w_subtitle: Middle ground between flexibility and immersion.
en.f8w_img_alt: 8-week semi-intensive yoga teacher training — students in practice
en.f8w_p1: Shorter timeframe: 8 weeks vs. 18 weeks — half the calendar blocking
en.f8w_p2: More intense than 18-week: 3-4 days/week creates deeper immersion and momentum
en.f8w_p3: Still possible to work part-time: 2-3 days off per week leaves room for part-time work
en.f8w_p4: Good flow: Enough time between sessions for depth, but short enough to maintain energy
en.f8w_p5: Summer timing: May/June start coincides with the holiday period for many
en.f8w_p6: Balanced pace: Not as pressured as 4-week, not as drawn out as 18-week
en.f8w_c1: Higher weekly time requirement: 20-25 hours/week incl. self-study — harder to combine with full-time work
en.f8w_c2: Compressed material: Anatomy and philosophy come faster — less time to digest complex topics
en.f8w_c3: Limited flexibility for absences: Missed days are harder to make up over just 8 weeks
en.f8w_c4: Fewer start dates: Only May/June 2026 — less flexibility in planning
en.f8w_c5: Requires strong planning: Family and social commitments must be closely coordinated
en.f4w_subtitle: Total immersion — full time, 7 days a week.
en.f4w_img_alt: 4-week intensive yoga teacher training — morning practice in the studio
en.f4w_d4: Hours per week: ~35-40 contact hours + self-study in the evening
en.f4w_d7: Accommodation: Optional — help with accommodation in Copenhagen offered
en.f4w_p1: Total immersion: You live, breathe and sleep yoga for 4 weeks — deeply transformative
en.f4w_p2: Fastest path to certification: Done in one month — from start to RYT-200
en.f4w_p3: Strong bonds: 4 weeks close together creates intense friendships and professional networks
en.f4w_p4: Perfect for internationals: Travel to Copenhagen, take the training, go home certified
en.f4w_p5: Clear focus: No distraction from work — you can give 100% to the training
en.f4w_p6: Summer season (July): Long bright days, outdoor practice opportunities, Copenhagen at its best
en.f4w_p7: Accommodation help: Yoga Bible helps find accommodation for international students
en.f4w_c1: Requires leave or resignation: You cannot work or study alongside — it is full time
en.f4w_c2: Physically demanding: 8-18 daily, 7 days a week — your body and mind are challenged
en.f4w_c3: Compressed learning: 200 hours of material in 4 weeks — limited time to digest philosophy and anatomy
en.f4w_c4: Risk of overload: Injuries, mental exhaustion and information overload are real risks
en.f4w_c5: Less time to internalize: You learn quickly, but deep anchoring often comes afterwards
en.f4w_c6: Limited flexibility: Illness or personal events are difficult to handle with 10% absence limit
en.f4w_c7: Accommodation cost: International students should budget an extra 8,000-15,000 DKK for 4 weeks of accommodation in Copenhagen
en.table_title: Overview: All Formats Side by Side
en.table_note: All formats use the same curriculum, the same instructors and award the same Yoga Alliance RYT-200 certification. The only difference is pace and time distribution.
en.s2_title: Part 2: When Should You Do Your YTT?
en.s2_subtitle: The timing of your yoga teacher training affects your experience more than most people realize. Here is an honest review of the seasons — both in Copenhagen and in general.
en.s2_img_alt: Copenhagen through four seasons — spring, summer, autumn, winter
en.spring_b1: Daylight grows dramatically: From 10 hours in March to 17 hours in May — you literally feel the energy rise week by week
en.spring_b2: The city awakens: The canals, parks and harbor baths come to life — great energy for a fresh start
en.spring_b3: Perfect for 18-week programs: Start in March, finish in July/August — your training follows the natural curve of light
en.spring_b4: Lower housing prices: Tourist season hasn't started yet — international students find accommodation more easily
en.spring_b5: Consideration: March-April can still be cold and grey. Mentally it requires patience
en.summer_b1: Longest days in Scandinavia: Up to 17 hours of daylight — energy for practice, socializing and exploration
en.summer_b2: Outdoor opportunities: Practice in parks, by canals, on rooftops — Copenhagen offers unique outdoor spaces
en.summer_b3: Best for 4-week intensive: The July cohort gives the ultimate summer experience in Copenhagen
en.summer_b4: Holiday atmosphere: Many Danes are on vacation — the city is calm but vibrant. Great for immersion
en.summer_b5: Consideration: High season = more expensive housing and more tourists. Book accommodation well in advance
en.summer_b6: Consideration: Hot days in a hot yoga studio require extra hydration and rest
en.autumn_b1: Inward energy: Daylight decreases — a natural match with philosophy, meditation and self-study
en.autumn_b2: The city is buzzing: Students, professionals and cultural life are in full swing after the summer break
en.autumn_b3: Good study conditions: Less distraction from outdoor activities — focus on the inner work
en.autumn_b4: Consideration: Dark afternoons (dark at 4 PM in November) can affect mood and energy
en.autumn_b5: Consideration: Rain and wind are frequent — cycling and transport can feel more cumbersome
en.winter_b1: Most introverted season: Short days and cold weather create a natural framework for deep inner work
en.winter_b2: Hygge season: Candles, warm drinks and intimate gatherings — Danish hygge culture is at its strongest
en.winter_b3: Cheapest accommodation: Lowest rental prices in Copenhagen — good for international budgets
en.winter_b4: Hot yoga feels extra good: Going from frost into a warm studio is a sensory experience in itself
en.winter_b5: Consideration: Down to 7 hours of daylight — requires conscious light therapy and vitamin D
en.winter_b6: Consideration: The winter cold is mild but wet — slippery and grey weather at times
en.season_rec_text: There is no objectively "best" season — it depends on your life situation, personality and goals. If you are energy-sensitive and thrive with light and warmth, spring/summer is ideal. If you seek depth, calm and introspection, autumn/winter may surprise you positively. The most important thing is that the timing fits your life — not that it looks perfect on Instagram.
en.s3_title: Part 3: Popular YTT Destinations — Honest Comparison
en.s3_subtitle: Thousands of people every year choose to do their yoga teacher training abroad. Here we review the most popular destinations with honest strengths and weaknesses — including Copenhagen, which we obviously know best, but try to evaluate just as critically.
en.bali_subtitle: The world's most popular YTT destination — and for good reason, but also with nuances.
en.bali_img_alt: Yoga in Bali — rice terraces and outdoor practice in Ubud
en.bali_s1: Price: YTT from $2,000-3,500 USD incl. accommodation and meals — significantly cheaper than the West
en.bali_s2: Spiritual atmosphere: Ubud and surroundings are steeped in yoga and wellness culture
en.bali_s3: Climate: Warm tropical climate year-round (25-33°C) — perfect for outdoor practice
en.bali_s4: Community: Enormous international yoga community with workshops, retreats and kirtan daily
en.bali_s5: Total immersion: You are "out of your everyday life" — no distraction from work or routines
en.bali_s6: Nature: Rice terraces, volcanoes, waterfalls and temples — visually inspiring
en.bali_w1: Quality variation: The market is oversaturated — many cheap YTTs with questionable quality and inexperienced teachers
en.bali_w2: Tourist bubble: The yoga scene in Ubud can feel commercial and disconnected from local culture
en.bali_w3: Isolation from reality: The training takes place in a "bubble" — difficult to transfer to teaching at home
en.bali_w4: Weather risk: Rainy season (November-March) can mean heavy rain and humidity daily
en.bali_w5: Health: Stomach troubles, dehydration and mosquito-borne diseases are real risks
en.bali_w6: Flight tickets: Copenhagen-Bali return typically costs 5,000-8,000 DKK
en.bali_w7: Not Yoga Alliance automatic: Not all Bali schools are registered — always verify the school's RYS status
en.india_subtitle: The birthplace of yoga. Rishikesh is the historical capital; Goa offers a more relaxed version.
en.india_img_alt: Yoga in Rishikesh, India — practice by the Ganges River
en.india_s1: Authenticity: Rishikesh is literally the "Yoga Capital of the World" — deeper spiritual context than any other destination
en.india_s2: Price: YTT from $1,200-2,500 USD incl. accommodation and meals — the cheapest serious destination
en.india_s3: Tradition: Access to masters and lineage-based teaching that goes back generations
en.india_s4: Cultural depth: Aarti ceremonies by the Ganges, ashrams, temples — yoga philosophy is living culture here
en.india_s5: Vegetarian food: Fresh, Ayurvedic food — often the healthiest you have ever eaten
en.india_w1: Culture shock: India is overwhelming for many Westerners — noise, traffic, poverty and bureaucracy
en.india_w2: Health risk: "Delhi belly" is almost unavoidable. Water quality and hygiene require constant attention
en.india_w3: Quality variation: Hundreds of schools in Rishikesh — many cheap ones with poor infrastructure and teacher quality
en.india_w4: Comfort: Basic accommodation (shared room, basic toilet) — not for everyone
en.india_w5: Climate: Rishikesh is extremely hot in summer (40°C+) and cool in winter. Best: October-March
en.india_w6: Visa and logistics: E-visa required, and internal transport (Delhi-Rishikesh) can be chaotic
en.india_w7: Teaching style: Traditional Indian schools may have a more rigid, guru-centered pedagogy — not everyone thrives with it
en.thailand_subtitle: Beach, jungle and affordable prices — a popular middle ground.
en.thailand_img_alt: Yoga in Thailand — beach practice on Koh Phangan
en.thailand_s1: Balance between comfort and price: YTT from $2,500-4,000 USD — better facilities than India, cheaper than Europe
en.thailand_s2: Amazing food: Thai cuisine is world-famous — fresh, healthy and affordable
en.thailand_s3: Infrastructure: Thailand has good infrastructure, fast wifi, modern facilities
en.thailand_s4: Nature: Beaches, jungle, hot springs — beautiful setting for a transformative experience
en.thailand_s5: Welcoming culture: Thailand is generally safe, friendly and easy to navigate for Westerners
en.thailand_w1: Yoga is imported: Thailand has no indigenous yoga tradition — teachers are typically Westerners living in Asia
en.thailand_w2: Party culture: Koh Phangan is also the Full Moon Party island — distractions are real
en.thailand_w3: Heat and humidity: Tropical heat (30-35°C) with high humidity can be exhausting
en.thailand_w4: Quality spread: Like Bali — many schools, varying quality. Research is important
en.thailand_w5: Monsoon: Rainy season (May-October) can mean daily heavy rain
en.costarica_subtitle: Central American jungle, sustainability and "Pura Vida" — the new popular destination.
en.costarica_img_alt: Yoga in Costa Rica — jungle retreat and outdoor practice
en.costarica_s1: Nature and biodiversity: Rainforest, volcanoes, beaches — one of the most scenic destinations
en.costarica_s2: Sustainability culture: Costa Rica is a world leader in green energy — matches yoga's ethical values
en.costarica_s3: Safety: The region is generally very safe for travelers
en.costarica_s4: English-friendly: Many Costa Ricans speak English — easy to navigate
en.costarica_w1: More expensive than Asia: YTT from $3,000-5,500 USD — plus expensive flights from Europe (8,000-12,000 DKK)
en.costarica_w2: Isolated location: Many retreats are remote in the jungle — fantastic for immersion, but difficult in emergencies
en.costarica_w3: Humidity and insects: Tropical climate with mosquitoes, spiders and high humidity
en.costarica_w4: Limited yoga tradition: Like Thailand, yoga here is imported — not a local tradition
en.costarica_w5: Time difference: 7-8 hours time difference from Denmark — affects contact with home
en.portugal_subtitle: Europe's yoga hotspot with sun, surf and Southern European charm — and no long flight.
en.portugal_img_alt: Yoga in Portugal — coastal landscape and outdoor practice in Algarve
en.portugal_s1: Short trip from Scandinavia: 3-4 hour flight, no time difference — easy to reach and cheap to fly
en.portugal_s2: Pleasant climate: Mild Mediterranean climate (15-30°C) — not too hot, not too cold
en.portugal_s3: EU country: No visa, EU health card applies, Danish SIM cards work
en.portugal_s4: Good facilities: Modern retreats with good standards — European comfort level
en.portugal_s5: Surf + yoga: Ericeira is Europe's surf mecca — a popular combination
en.portugal_w1: More expensive than Asia: YTT from €2,500-4,500 — closer to Danish prices
en.portugal_w2: Less yoga tradition: The yoga scene is relatively new and primarily driven by expats
en.portugal_w3: Seasonal: Winter is mild but rainy — best in spring/summer
en.portugal_w4: Tourist pressure: Popular areas (Lagos, Ericeira) can feel overrun
en.portugal_w5: Less community after YTT: When you go home, your yoga community is in another country
en.cph_subtitle: Your own city — or one of Europe's most liveable cities, if you are traveling here.
en.cph_img_alt: Yoga teacher training in Copenhagen — practice in Christianshavn studio
en.cph_s1: No travel costs (locals): Live at home, eat at home, keep your job — no extra costs beyond the training
en.cph_s2: Teach locally from day 1: Real-class practice in Copenhagen studios — you build your network where you will teach
en.cph_s3: Continue your life: Family, friends, job and routines continue — the training integrates into your life, not the other way around
en.cph_s4: Local network: Your fellow students are your future colleagues and friends — in your city
en.cph_s5: Flexible formats: 4, 8 or 18 weeks — adapted to your life situation
en.cph_s6: European city quality: World-class gastronomically, culturally and architecturally — more than just yoga
en.cph_s7: Healthcare system: Danish/EU health coverage — no worries about travel insurance
en.cph_s8: Safety: Consistently ranked as one of the world's safest cities
en.cph_s9: Yoga Alliance registered: RYS-200 status with Yoga Alliance — guaranteed certification standard
en.cph_w1: Not exotic: No rice terraces, tropical beaches or ashram atmosphere — it is a Northern European city
en.cph_w2: Weather: Cold and dark November-February, unpredictable weather generally — not dream weather for outdoor yoga
en.cph_w3: More expensive overall (for internationals): Housing in Copenhagen (8,000-15,000 DKK/month) + living costs are significantly more expensive than Asia
en.cph_w4: Everyday distractions: You are in your familiar surroundings — the temptation to prioritize work, friends and parties can undermine immersion
en.cph_w5: Less "transformative bubble": You don't get the total separation from everyday life that a retreat format offers
en.cph_w6: Limited yoga scene compared to major cities: Copenhagen has a good yoga community, but it is smaller than London, Berlin or Los Angeles
en.retreat_title: The Important Question: Retreat vs. Home City
en.retreat_subtitle: The deepest question is not "Bali or Copenhagen" — it is whether you need to leave your everyday life to immerse yourself, or whether you want to integrate the training into your existing life.
en.retreat_r1: You find it hard to prioritize yourself in your everyday life
en.retreat_r2: You want a total "reset" and fresh start
en.retreat_r3: You don't plan to teach locally afterwards
en.retreat_r4: You want to travel AND train at the same time
en.retreat_r5: You thrive with isolation and new surroundings
en.retreat_r6: You have budget for travel, accommodation and training combined
en.home_r1: You want to teach locally and build a network from day 1
en.home_r2: You have a job, family or commitments you cannot leave
en.home_r3: You want to integrate yoga into your existing life — not escape from it
en.home_r4: You want real-class teaching experience in actual studios
en.home_r5: You prefer a gradual format (18 weeks) over a 4-week intensive
en.home_r6: You want to save on travel and accommodation costs
en.s4_title: Part 4: How Do You Choose the Right One?
en.s4_subtitle: Use this framework to structure your decision. There is no "right" or "wrong" — only what fits your life best right now.
en.choose1_desc: Do you want to teach professionally? Prioritize a school with real-class practice and a local network. Is it personal development? A retreat may be more suitable. Both? Choose a format that balances immersion and practice.
en.choose2_desc: Job: Can you take time off? The 18-week weekend suits full-time. The 4-week requires leave. Family: Do you have responsibilities keeping you home? Finances: Do you have a buffer for 1-4 months of reduced income?
en.choose3_desc: Slow learner? The 18-week format allows time for digestion. Intensive learner? The 4-week suits you better. Introvert? Total immersion can be overwhelming. Extrovert? You likely thrive in an intensive group environment.
en.choose4_desc: Total cost of ownership: Training price + accommodation + flights + lost income + food + visa. A "cheap" YTT in Bali with flights, accommodation and 4 weeks of lost salary can be more expensive than a YTT in your home city where you keep your job.
en.choose5_desc: Always check: Is the school Yoga Alliance registered (RYS)? Who are the instructors and what is their background? Are there reviews from former students? How many hours are contact hours vs. self-study? Is real-class teaching practice included?
en.choose6_desc: The most important question: What happens the day after you are certified? Do you have a network, a studio to teach at, a community to return to? Or do you go home to an empty calendar? The best YTT is the one that gives you a sustainable start — not just a diploma.
en.insight_p1: Many newly graduated yoga teachers experience a "post-YTT dip": they come home from a retreat, filled with inspiration — but have no network, no studio and no plan. The transformative experience fades quickly when everyday life returns.
en.insight_p2: This is not an argument against retreat formats — it is an argument for having a plan for what happens afterwards. Whether you choose Bali, Rishikesh or Copenhagen, the most important investment is not the training itself — it is the first 6 months after.
en.faq1_q: What is the difference in curriculum between the formats?
en.faq1_a: None. All Yoga Bible formats (18-week, 8-week and 4-week) use exactly the same curriculum, the same manuals, the same exams and the same instructors. The difference is solely pace and time distribution. The certification (RYT-200) is identical.
en.faq2_a: In special cases (e.g. illness, job change or family circumstances) you can request to switch to another format or complete with the next cohort. Contact us early and we will find a solution.
en.faq3_q: Is a YTT in Bali or India "worth less" than one in Denmark?
en.faq3_a: No. A Yoga Alliance RYT-200 certification is the same regardless of destination — it is an international standard. The quality of the training itself depends on the school, the instructors and the program — not on geographic location. There are excellent schools everywhere, and there are poor schools everywhere. Research is crucial.
en.faq4_q: What is the total cost of doing a YTT abroad vs. Copenhagen?
en.faq4_a: For a Dane with a full-time job: A 4-week YTT in Bali typically costs ~$3,000 USD (YTT) + ~6,000 DKK (flights) + ~4 weeks lost salary (~50,000-80,000 DKK). Total: 75,000-110,000 DKK. An 18-week weekend YTT in Copenhagen: 25,500 DKK + 0 DKK in lost salary (you keep your job). Total: 25,500 DKK. Numbers are approximate and individual — but the difference is significant.
en.faq5_q: Is 4 weeks enough to learn yoga?
en.faq5_a: 200 hours is 200 hours — whether spread over 4 or 18 weeks. But yoga teacher training is a beginning, not an end. Regardless of format, you should expect 6-12 months of continued practice and teaching before you feel confident and competent as a teacher.
en.faq6_q: What is the difference between RYT-200 from different schools?
en.faq6_a: Yoga Alliance sets minimum requirements (200 hours distributed across specific categories). But schools vary greatly in quality, depth, teaching staff and practice. An RYT-200 from a serious school with experienced teachers and real-class practice is a stronger qualification than one from a cheap school without practical teaching experience — even if the diploma looks the same.
en.faq7_q: Can I do my YTT in summer and start teaching in autumn?
en.faq7_a: Yes — that is a popular plan. July 4-week intensive > August rest and personal practice > September start teaching. Many studios and fitness centers recruit instructors for the autumn season. Yoga Bible helps with networking and recommendations to local studios.
en.faq8_q: Is it necessary to have a YTT to teach yoga?
en.faq8_a: Legally: No. Yoga is not a regulated profession in Denmark (or most countries). Practically: Yes. Most studios, fitness centers and insurance companies require RYT-200 as a minimum. And more importantly: a thorough education protects your students from injuries and gives you credibility.
en.cta_text: See our three formats in detail, or contact us for personal guidance.
